subject: First Aid & Safety
title: The Dose Makes the Poison
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Dose Makes the Poison.

hook.text: Water can kill you. Arsenic can heal you. The difference is always the dose.

buildup.text: Paracelsus wrote in 1538 that every substance is a poison—only the dose determines the effect.

discovery.text: Even oxygen becomes toxic at high concentrations, damaging lungs and causing seizures.

twist.text: Botulinum toxin—the deadliest substance known—is used safely as Botox in tiny amounts.

climax.text: Understanding dose-response relationships is the foundation of toxicology and safe medicine.

punchline.text: Nothing is safe. Nothing is poison. Only the dose decides.

quiz.question: Who first stated that 'the dose makes the poison'?
